title: Query Cost Estimation
implementationNotes: QueryCostEstimator implemented with estimateMethod(), estimateAllMethods(), recommendMethod(). SearchManager.autoSearch() and search_auto tool added for automatic algorithm selection.
impact: Enables intelligent query optimization, progress estimation for long queries, and automatic search algorithm selection

algorithmSelection.target: Automatic optimal algorithm selection based on query characteristics

progressPrediction.target: Accurate progress % based on estimated vs actual work done

tasks.title: Create QueryCostEstimator Class
tasks.description: Create a QueryCostEstimator class that analyzes queries and graph state to estimate execution cost. Provides estimates in terms of entity iterations, index lookups, and expected latency.
tasks.implementationNotes: Implemented in src/search/QueryCostEstimator.ts with estimateMethod(), estimateAllMethods(), recommendMethod() methods. Returns QueryCostEstimate with complexity, recommendations, and estimated time.

implementation.purpose: Provide cost estimates for different search operations based on query complexity and graph size. Used for algorithm selection, progress tracking, and query optimization hints.
keyDecisions: Cost expressed as CostEstimate object with multiple metrics
Supports all search types: basic, ranked, boolean, fuzzy, semantic
Uses graph statistics for accurate estimation
Provides algorithm recommendation based on query characteristics

stepByStep.details: Create the new file in the search directory

stepByStep.code:
/**
 * Query Cost Estimator
 *
 * Analyzes queries and graph state to estimate execution cost.
 * Used for algorithm selection, progress tracking, and optimization hints.
 *
 * @module search/QueryCostEstimator
 */

import type { GraphStats, BooleanQueryNode } from '../types/types.js';

acceptanceCriteria: CostEstimate interface with all fields defined
QueryAnalysis interface with all fields defined
estimateSearch() returns accurate cost estimates for all algorithms
Cost constants are realistic (calibrated to benchmarks)
Class has comprehensive JSDoc with examples

tasks.title: Integrate QueryCostEstimator into SearchManager
tasks.description: Add getCostEstimate() method to SearchManager that uses QueryCostEstimator to provide cost estimates for any search. Also add automatic algorithm selection option.
tasks.implementationNotes: SearchManager.autoSearch() method implemented. Uses QueryCostEstimator to select best search method and returns AutoSearchResult with method, reason, and results.

implementation.purpose: Expose cost estimation to users via SearchManager and enable automatic algorithm selection for optimal performance.
keyDecisions: Add searchAuto() method that uses recommendation
implementation.existingCodeContext: SearchManager.ts orchestrates all search operations. Add cost estimator as private property and new public methods.

stepByStep.action: Add private cost estimator property after existing properties
Add searchAuto method for automatic algorithm selection

stepByStep.details: Ensure Entity is imported from types
Run: npx vitest run tests/unit/search/SearchManager.test.ts (if exists) or integration tests

acceptanceCriteria: searchAuto() method added with automatic algorithm selection
searchAuto returns entities, algorithm used, and cost estimate
All search algorithms supported in searchAuto

tasks.title: Add search_auto MCP Tool
tasks.description: Add a new search_auto MCP tool that exposes automatic algorithm selection to MCP clients. Returns results with algorithm used and cost metrics.
tasks.implementationNotes: search_auto tool added to toolDefinitions.ts and toolHandlers.ts. Calls ctx.searchManager.autoSearch() and returns results with selected method.

implementation.purpose: Expose automatic algorithm selection via MCP so clients can leverage intelligent query optimization without manual algorithm choice.
keyDecisions: Returns: entities, algorithm used, cost estimate
Parameters similar to search_nodes but without algorithm choice
implementation.existingCodeContext: toolDefinitions.ts has all 54 tool schemas. toolHandlers.ts dispatches to managers. Add after search_nodes tool.

stepByStep.action: Find the search_nodes tool definition (in SEARCH TOOLS section)
stepByStep.details: Search for 'search_nodes' to find the search tools section

stepByStep.action: Add search_auto tool definition after search_nodes

stepByStep.details: Search for 'case "search_nodes"' to find the search handlers section

acceptanceCriteria: search_auto tool definition added to toolDefinitions.ts
Tool accepts query, tags, minImportance, maxImportance, entityType, limit
Response includes entities, algorithm, costEstimate, count

tasks.title: Create Query Cost Estimator Tests
tasks.description: Create comprehensive unit tests for QueryCostEstimator and integration tests for SearchManager.searchAuto().
tasks.implementationNotes: Tests created in tests/unit/search/QueryCostEstimator.test.ts covering estimateMethod, estimateAllMethods, recommendMethod, and custom options.

implementation.purpose: Verify cost estimation accuracy, algorithm recommendations, and automatic search selection.
keyDecisions: Test cost estimation for each algorithm

acceptanceCriteria: Tests for analyzeQuery - simple, field specifiers, boolean, fuzzy, ranking
Tests for estimateSearch - basic, fuzzy comparison, index usage, complexity
Tests for recommendAlgorithm - each algorithm recommendation case

successCriteria: All four tasks completed with passing tests
QueryCostEstimator class with estimateSearch, analyzeQuery, recommendAlgorithm
Cost estimates include entityIterations, estimatedMs, complexity, explanation
Algorithm recommendations accurate for query types
notes: Cost constants should be calibrated based on actual benchmark results
Confidence decreases for larger graphs and more complex queries
searchAuto provides easy API for clients that don't want to choose algorithms
Future enhancement: Add actual timing feedback to improve estimates over time
search_auto tool is especially useful for LLM clients like Claude
